title: Intensive Family Reunification Services * Substance Use Disorder Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Intensive Family Reunification Services * Substance Use Disorder Issues 1 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Native American Community Substance Use Disorder Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF WILLAMETTE FAMILY INC WILLAMETTE FAMILY SUNRISE HOUSE & FAMILIES IN RECOVERY FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers two family programs for those in residential/outpatient substance addiction treatment: -Family Reunion Program for DHS-referred families: Program provides services to assist with preventing children from entering the foster care system and/or being reunited with their families after having been in foster care. Services include family advocacy, visitation, home visits, co-residency for children through age 5 whose mothers are in residential treatment, parent education, and mentoring. -Family Services: During residential treatment, clients have access to family advocates who assist families with parenting-related needs. Eligibility: Families in which one or both parents are in substance abuse and/or mental health treatment through Willamette Family. Priority to families where children are at risk of removal from family to be placed into foster care, or who are in foster care and are being reunited with their parents. Hours: No standard hours (541) 762-4300 Visit Website More Details No Mapping Information Available
